reviewer.basis: candidate is pure C: array indexing + arithmetic only; zero regfix/asmfix rules, zero __asm__, zero register asm() pins, zero volatile/alias coercion. audit_asm_cheats.py --func reports nothing.

rejected_forms: s16[][2] indexing without cached base -> recomputes lui/addu per access (score 16-21)
shift arg0 in place + temp_a0 late (variant e/i/j) -> score 15-17 (breaks bltz delay-slot fill AND addu operand order)
char* base + arg0*4 cast (variant g) -> bi=47 extra insn, score 16
precompute temp_a0 WITHOUT base-first ptr -> score 8 (wrong addu operand order)

sessions.note: Lowered floor 21->4. base=&D_8009AD1C[0][0] computed BEFORE index fixes addu operand order (v0=index,v1=base). Remaining 4: target keeps s0=arg0 unshifted through lh/bltz, shifts in bltz DELAY SLOT, computes +1 late; GCC schedules the full (arg0<<1)+1 together early instead. Register-pin (asm($16)) wall.
